question.text: Which of the following is NOT a role of a merchant banker in M&A transactions?

answer.text: Approving the scheme of arrangement on behalf of NCLT
Providing fairness opinion on valuation of assets and shares
Preparation and circulation of information memoranda

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Merchant bankers perform information memoranda preparation, deal structuring, valuation, due diligence, fairness opinions, and liaison activities. However, approving schemes of arrangement is the function of NCLT (National Company Law Tribunal), not the merchant banker. **The Trap:** Since merchant bankers are involved in virtually every aspect of an M&A deal, candidates may assume they also have approval authority. But MBs are advisors and facilitators — judicial/quasi-judicial approvals rest with NCLT. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests the distinction between advisory roles (merchant banker) and regulatory/judicial roles (SEBI, NCLT) extensively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A merchant banker's value lies in preparation and process management — never confuse advisory authority with approval authority. Understanding this boundary prevents compliance errors.
